item.title: Reece, Bower and Fihaki to start for Crusaders against Blues this Saturday
item.description: Navitalai Naivalurua · 04/06/2026 Three players with links to Fiji will line up for the Crusaders when they host the Blues this Saturday in the Shop N Save Super Rugby Pacific qualifying final.
item.content:encoded:
Navitalai Naivalurua · 04/06/2026

Three players with links to Fiji will line up for the Crusaders when they host the Blues this Saturday in the Shop N Save Super Rugby Pacific qualifying final.

George Bower will start at prop, while Sevu Reece and Chay Fihaki retain their places on the wings.

Taha Kemara returns to start at first five-eighth, while Braydon Ennor and Tahlor Cahill are back from injury and have been named among the reserves, adding valuable depth heading into the finals.

Meanwhile, tickets for the Crusaders-Blues clash were snapped up in just 20 minutes, marking a fifth consecutive sell-out at the new stadium and highlighting the strong appetite for finals rugby.

The Crusaders will face the Blues at 4.35pm on Saturday.

In the other qualifying final, the Hurricanes will take on the Brumbies at 7.05pm tomorrow, while the Chiefs will host the Reds at 7.05pm on Saturday.

item.title: Raketekete set for dream Drua debut against Waratahs this Saturday
item.description: A dream years in the making is about to become reality for 22-year-old Ruth Raketekete, who is set to make her debut for the McDonald's Fiji Drua Women in their Super Rugby Women's season opener against the NSW Waratahs this Saturday in Sydney. The utility back from Noco in Rewa, who also has maternal links to Wainibuka in Tailevu, says she is feeling both excited and nervous ahead of her first ap
item.content:encoded:
A dream years in the making is about to become reality for 22-year-old Ruth Raketekete, who is set to make her debut for the McDonald's Fiji Drua Women in their Super Rugby Women's season opener against the NSW Waratahs this Saturday in Sydney.

The utility back from Noco in Rewa, who also has maternal links to Wainibuka in Tailevu, says she is feeling both excited and nervous ahead of her first appearance for the Drua.

Raketekete says earning selection for the opening round is a privilege that comes with great responsibility, acknowledging the hard work required to secure a place in the squad.

The young back is among several players set to make their Super Rugby Women's debuts as the Drua begin their 2026 campaign against the defending champions.

The match will kick off at 4.05pm on Saturday at the Leichhardt Oval in Sydney.

item.title: Yamal races to recover ahead of Spain's World Cup campaign
item.description: Spain and Barcelona teenage star Lamine Yamal is continuing his recovery from a hamstring injury ahead of the 2026 FIFA World Cup, with hopes growing that he will be fit for Spain's opening match. The 18-year-old winger, who emerged as one of football's brightest stars following his impressive performances at UEFA Euro 2024, has not played since sustaining the injury during Barcelona's victory ove
item.content:encoded:
Spain and Barcelona teenage star Lamine Yamal is continuing his recovery from a hamstring injury ahead of the 2026 FIFA World Cup, with hopes growing that he will be fit for Spain's opening match.

The 18-year-old winger, who emerged as one of football's brightest stars following his impressive performances at UEFA Euro 2024, has not played since sustaining the injury during Barcelona's victory over Celta Vigo in April.

Initial fears suggested Yamal may have suffered a serious hamstring tear, raising concerns over his availability for the World Cup.

However, Barcelona later confirmed that while the youngster would miss the remainder of the domestic season, he was expected to recover in time for the tournament.

Despite fitness concerns, Spain coach Luis de la Fuente included Yamal in his 26-player World Cup squad , highlighting the youngster's importance to La Roja's title ambitions.

There was further encouragement for Spanish fans in late May when Yamal posted footage of himself back in training and working with the ball at Barcelona's training base, suggesting his rehabilitation is progressing well.

Reports indicate that Spain and Barcelona medical staff have remained in close contact throughout his recovery, with suggestions that Yamal could be eased back into action later in the group stage rather than being risked in Spain's opening matches.

De la Fuente remains optimistic that the teenage sensation will play a role during the tournament and has hinted that Yamal could initially be used as an impact substitute if required.

Yamal is widely regarded as one of the most exciting young talents in world football, with his dribbling ability, creativity and match-winning performances making him a key figure in Spain's hopes of lifting the World Cup trophy.

The teenager, who will turn 19 just days before the World Cup final, will be eager to overcome his injury setback and showcase his talents on football's biggest stage.

Spain opens its World Cup campaign against Cape Verde on 16th June before facing Saudi Arabia on 22nd June and Uruguay on the 27th in Group H.

Spain has won the FIFA World Cup once, claiming its only men's title in 2010 when it defeated the Netherlands 1–0 in the final in South Africa.

Meanwhile, the 2026 FIFA World Cup will kick off next Friday and run until 20th July.

item.title: Barrett ruled out as Blues name squad for Crusaders clash
item.description: Navitalai Naivalurua · 04/06/2026 The Blues will head into this week's 2026 Shop N Save Super Rugby Pacific qualifying final against the Crusaders without Beauden Barrett, who remains sidelined with a quad injury.
item.content:encoded:
Navitalai Naivalurua · 04/06/2026

The Blues will head into this week's 2026 Shop N Save Super Rugby Pacific qualifying final against the Crusaders without Beauden Barrett, who remains sidelined with a quad injury.

The Blues have named their 23-member squad, with Stephen Perofeta starting at flyhalf.

Prop Ofa Tu’ungafasi is set to become the most-capped player in club history and will make his 165th appearance for the Blues, surpassing former club legend Keven Mealamu's record of 164 appearances.

The Blues have received another boost this week with the return of captain Patrick Tuipulotu and hooker Bradley Slater.

Meanwhile, All Black Dalton Papali’i and Māori All Blacks captain Kurt Eklund remain sidelined.

The Blues will take on the Crusaders at 4.35pm on Saturday.

In the other qualifying final, the Hurricanes will face the Brumbies at 7.05pm tomorrow, while the Chiefs will host the Reds at 7.05pm on Saturday.

Reserves: 16. Eli Oudenryn (1), 17. Mason Tupaea (22), 18. Flyn Yates (4), 19. Josh Beehre (38), 20. Hoskins Sotutu (90), 21. Finlay Christie (82), 22. Corey Evans (28), 23. Payton Spencer (3)

item.title: Kulas suffer heartbreaking 3-2 defeat to Hong Kong
item.description: The Fiji Kulas fell short in a 3-2 loss to Hong Kong in the first of two FIFA Women's International Friendlies in Hong Kong last night. The hosts strengthened their grip on the contest with two quick goals early in the second half to take a 3-0 lead and put the Kulas under significant pressure.
item.content:encoded:
The Fiji Kulas fell short in a 3-2 loss to Hong Kong in the first of two FIFA Women's International Friendlies in Hong Kong last night.

The hosts strengthened their grip on the contest with two quick goals early in the second half to take a 3-0 lead and put the Kulas under significant pressure.

However, Fiji responded strongly, with Narieta Leba scoring the team's first goal before Elesi Tabunase added a second to reduce the deficit and set up an exciting finish.

Despite the late surge, the Kulas were unable to find an equaliser.

Head coach Nicola Demaine says the match provided the challenging test the team had been seeking against opposition from outside the Oceania region.

She notes that the side had started cautiously as they adjusted to the unfamiliar opposition but grew in confidence as the game progressed , highlighting the importance of exposure to higher-level international competition.

Demaine acknowledges the disappointment of conceding three goals but says the team's ability to fight back from a three-goal deficit reflected its determination and resilience.

The coach also welcomes the opportunity to give all 18 available players game time under FIFA's updated substitution regulations, with Ulamila Rasovasova making her senior international debut.

Demaine believes the lessons learned from the defeat would help the Kulas deliver a more consistent performance in the second and final friendly.

Fiji will meet Hong Kong again tomorrow in the final match of the FIFA international series.

item.title: Ten to debut for Fiji Drua Women in first 2026 Super W match against Waratahs
item.description: Navitalai Naivalurua · 04/06/2026 Ten players are set to make their Super Rugby Women's debut for the McDonald's Fiji Drua Women's side when they take on the Waratahs this weekend.
item.content:encoded:
Navitalai Naivalurua · 04/06/2026

Ten players are set to make their Super Rugby Women's debut for the McDonald's Fiji Drua Women's side when they take on the Waratahs this weekend.

Nadi's Karavaki Lutumaibau will make her debut at lock, while Varanisese Qoro will start at flyhalf. Aqela Raitubu has been named on the wing and Ivamere Rokowati at centre.

Zipporah Sorokacika, Keri Lawavou, Vatiseva Cavuru, Ruth Raketekete and Michella'e Stolz have been named on the bench.

The starting XV sees Karalaini Naisewa teaming up with Tiana Robanakadavu and captain Bitila Tawake in the front row.

Nadi native Mereoni Nakesa will partner Lutumaibau at lock, while 18-year-old Carletta Yee lines up at blindside flanker and Nunia Daunimoala starts at openside flanker.

Vice-captain Alfreda Fisher starts at number eight.

Evivi Senikarivi will partner Qoro in the halves, while Raitubu and vice-captain Kolora Lomani are on the wings.

Josivini Naihamu starts at inside centre, with Rokowati at outside centre, while Litiana Vueti is at fullback.

The Fiji Drua Women will take on the Waratahs at 4.05pm on Saturday.

In another match, the Reds will face the Brumbies at 3.05pm on Sunday.

item.title: Manchester City tops FIFA World Cup player list as 48 squads confirmed
item.description: Manchester City will have more players at the FIFA World Cup than any other club, with a record 19 players representing 12 different countries at the tournament. Despite missing out on the Premier League title, City's players remain among the most sought-after on the international stage.
item.content:encoded:
Manchester City will have more players at the FIFA World Cup than any other club, with a record 19 players representing 12 different countries at the tournament.

Despite missing out on the Premier League title, City's players remain among the most sought-after on the international stage.

The champions of Germany, England, France and Spain follow closely behind, while Arsenal will have 16 players representing 10 nations at the World Cup.

Italian champions Inter Milan will have only seven players at the tournament, largely due to Italy's failure to qualify.

Among Premier League clubs, only the top three teams from the 2025/26 season have more World Cup representatives than Conference League winners Crystal Palace, who have 12 players selected.

Newly promoted Sunderland have also made a strong impression, matching Chelsea and Liverpool with 11 World Cup representatives, highlighting the club's extensive international recruitment.

Meanwhile, Brentford, Everton and Leeds will each have only four players involved at the World Cup, allowing the majority of their squads a longer off-season break before preparations begin for the new campaign.

All 48 World Cup squads have now been confirmed, with fans eagerly awaiting the tournament as the world's best players prepare to represent their countries on football's biggest stage.

Meanwhile, the FIFA World Cup will kick off next Friday and run until 20th July.

item.title: Boost for Drua Women as Imeri Nai returns from concussion
item.description: There is encouraging news for the McDonald's Fijian Drua Women ahead of the start of their 2026 Super Rugby Women's campaign, with flyhalf Imeri Nai returning to training after recovering from a concussion. Head Coach Mike Legge confirmed to fijivillage News that Nai is back in training following the injury she sustained during the Drua Women's pre-season trial against the NSW Waratahs Women in Su
item.content:encoded:
There is encouraging news for the McDonald's Fijian Drua Women ahead of the start of their 2026 Super Rugby Women's campaign, with flyhalf Imeri Nai returning to training after recovering from a concussion.

Head Coach Mike Legge confirmed to fijivillage News that Nai is back in training following the injury she sustained during the Drua Women's pre-season trial against the NSW Waratahs Women in Suva.

Nai was forced from the field during the match after suffering a concussion and has spent the past few weeks on the sidelines as she progressed through her recovery.

Her return comes as the Drua Women prepare to launch their Super Rugby Women's season this Saturday against the defending champion Waratahs at Leichhardt Oval in Sydney.

Legge has named an exciting squad featuring a blend of experienced internationals and emerging talent.

Several players are set to make their official Super Rugby Women's debuts for the Drua, including Vodafone Fijiana 15s representatives Varanisese Qoro and Ivamere Rokowati, along with Nadroga lock Karavaki Lutumaibau and Nasinu standout Aqela Raitubu.

The starting 15 sees the most capped Drua player of all-time (25 appearances) Karalaini Naisewa (1) at loosehead prop, with Tiana Robanakadavu (3) at tighthead. Captain Bitila Tawake (2) will start at hooker. In the middle row, Vunamoli, Nadi native MereoniNakesa (4) partners the the debuting KaravakiLutumaibau (5). The squad’s youngest member, 18-year-old Carletta Yee (6) lines up at blindside flanker with Nunia Daunimoala (7) at openside. Vice-captain Alfreda Fisher (8) starts the match at number 8. In the backline, the experienced Evivi Senikarivi (9) gets the nod to start at halfback, partnering VaraniseseQoro (10) who is at flyhalf. Flyer Aqela Raitubu (11) starts on the left wing with vice-captain Kolora Lomani (14) on the right wing. Josivini Naihamu (12) starts the match at inside centre with Ivamere Rokowati (13) at outside centre. Litiana Vueti (15) has been handed the fullback duties for the match. For impact this week, Keleni Marawa (16) is backup hooker, Zipporah Sorokacika (17) is loosehead, with Keri Lawavou (18) covering the tighthead prop position. Lanky second rower Vilisi Tivalele (19) will cover for the locks and Salaseini Railumu (20) backs up the loose forward trio. Vatiseva Cavuru (21) will cover the halves with Ruth Raketekete (22) covering the centres. Michella’e Stolz (23) completes matchday squad covering the back three.

item.title: Blues are the arch enemy and will be a threat - Crusaders coach
item.description: Crusaders coach Rob Penney says anyone claiming they will "roll" through the Blues in Saturday's qualifying final is underestimati

item.description: The All Whites’ worst result in nine years has left them with plenty to digest and plenty to work on less than a fortnight out fro

item.description: A new bid to rescue Moana Pasifika has emerged, with a consortium led by a Los Angeles-based tech multimillionaire exploring plans

item.description: The Fiji Airways Men's National 7s side will open its Bordeaux 7s campaign against Kenya at 12.44am on Saturday.Fiji is currently

item.title: Nabenu set for Rewa, Ali lodges transfer request to Lautoka
item.description: New Zealand-based Extra Bula FC defender Semi Nabenu is set to join Rewa FC after completing the mandatory 30-day stand-down perio
